concerns.text: Tasks T030, T031, and T032 introduce 'topological attractor analysis' (Lyapunov/correlation dimension) and 'adaptive/degenerative' classification labels. These deliverables are NOT present in spec.md (FR-003 mandates only LZC and Permutation Entropy) nor authorized by plan.md. This is unapproved scope creep that violates the Single Source of Truth (Constitution Principle IV) and creates ghost requirements.

Task T022 requires the final report to 'explicitly discuss the distinction between adaptive and degenerative complexity changes.' Since 'adaptive/degenerative' is not a defined concept in spec.md (FR-004/FR-006) or plan.md, this task forces the generation of content based on an unapproved hypothesis, violating the requirement that all report content must trace to spec/plan mandates.

Task T001 is marked with a failure note ('FAILED: unspecified') regarding directory hierarchy evidence, yet the task description remains unchanged. A task that has been explicitly flagged as failing its verification criteria without a revised, verifiable description violates the constraint that every task must describe a concretely-verifiable action.

Tasks T014 and T015 are marked with failure notes ('FAILED: unspecified') regarding missing output artifacts (lzc_metrics.csv, pe_metrics.csv) in the review section, yet they remain active implementation steps without a re-planned decomposition. This violates the constraint that tasks must guarantee artifact generation to satisfy FR-003.

Task T031 defines a 'classification heuristic' to label participants as 'adaptive' or 'degenerative.' Spec.md (FR-004) and Plan.md only authorize reporting correlation coefficients and p-values. Generating categorical labels for participants is a deliverable not defined in the spec, constituting a silent relaxation of the defined output scope.
